source.name: User distilled premium web UI prompt set
source.license_notes: Rules distilled from user-provided prompts for personal knowledge base use. Do not copy external media assets directly.

visual_traits: subtle translucent cards and strong CTA glass
inset highlight and controlled outer shadow
blurred background media or ambient scenery visible through surfaces
rounded full controls and rounded 2xl/3xl cards
layout_rules: Use subtle glass for navbars, badges, chips, cards, and stat blocks.
Use strong glass only for primary CTAs, emphasized controls, selected states, and hero surfaces.
Keep strong glass away from dense data grids and hundreds of repeated rows.
Glass needs meaningful background input; use media, ambient scenes, or gentle gradients behind it.
Keep primary text on stable tinted glass surfaces and avoid busy detail under small text.
accessibility_notes: Check contrast after blur and media overlays are composited.
Do not use color glow as the only state indicator.
Avoid low-contrast gray text on glass.
implementation_hints: Subtle glass background: rgba(255,255,255,0.01) to rgba(255,255,255,0.12).
Subtle glass blur: backdrop-filter blur(4px) to blur(16px), with -webkit-backdrop-filter.
Strong glass blur: blur(40px) to blur(50px), stronger inset highlight, subtle outer shadow, and gradient rim border.
Use ::before with inset:0, border-radius:inherit, 1px-1.5px padding, linear-gradient rim, and mask-composite exclude/xor where supported.
Use rounded-full for nav/badge/CTA and rounded-2xl or rounded-3xl for cards.
Do not stop at bg-white/10 backdrop-blur; add rim, highlight, fallback, and controlled layering.

quality.notes: Distilled from multiple detailed React/Tailwind premium landing page prompts covering glassmorphism, video hero sections, typography, motion, SaaS, portfolio, and editorial layouts.

retrieval.ai_summary: Premium Liquid Glass implementation rules: use two glass levels, subtle translucent surfaces for nav/cards and strong glass for CTA/selected controls; add gradient pseudo-element borders, inset highlights, fallback colors, and contrast checks. For high-end landing pages and cinematic UI, Liquid Glass is a priority material; for dense CRUD/table tasks it remains optional.
